concept.rationale: Figure B correctly models the MRF as a true 3D graph with distinct pairwise edges and accurately demonstrates the core concept of a Markov blanket, whereas Figure A fakes the 3D perspective by shearing nodes in 2D space and uses single continuous cylinders for entire rows of edges.

aggregator.explanation: Figure B is chosen as the overall winner because it constructs a genuine 3D lattice and accurately models the core concepts of the MRF and Markov blanket while offering superior interactive features. Although Figure A exhibits better label placement and visual faithfulness to the reference, its reliance on a hardcoded 2D isometric projection significantly limits its geometric and conceptual depth.

geometry.rationale: Figure B expertly replicates the canonical MATLAB stem3 view, including the exact camera azimuth and axis placement on the leftmost bounding box edge, whereas Figure A misplaces the camera and axes.

interactivity.rationale: Figure A features a functional normalization toggle button that animates the filter kernel to demonstrate the difference between the unnormalized and normalized box filter, whereas Figure B lacks developer-built parameter controls.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure A preserves the original's orientation with the vertical axis on the left and the 'm' axis on the front-left, whereas Figure B incorrectly mirrors the layout by placing the vertical axis on the right.

labels.rationale: Figure A correctly positions the Z-axis label on the left matching the reference, whereas Figure B incorrectly places it on the right.

concept.rationale: Figure A includes an interactive toggle to normalize the kernel (demonstrating unit DC gain), which actively and accurately teaches a crucial concept about averaging filters that a static plot misses.

aggregator.explanation: Four out of five dimension agents strongly favor Figure A due to its interactive normalization toggle and superior conceptual depth. Furthermore, Figure A correctly aligns the vertical axis and labels on the left, whereas Figure B incorrectly mirrors the layout.

geometry.rationale: Figure A builds a true 3D geometric construction of the scene, whereas Figure B creates a 2D drawing mapped onto a flat plane in 3D space, which automatically loses.

interactivity.rationale: Figure B features continuous sliders that dynamically update the scene to demonstrate how depth and 3D velocity magnitude affect 2D image velocity, which is far more pedagogically valuable than Figure A's simple visibility toggle.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure B much more accurately replicates the perspective, line thicknesses, arrowhead styles, and relative proportions of the original figure compared to Figure A.

labels.rationale: Figure B has better label placement, specifically for 'p' and the 'p-dot' vector, which are positioned much closer to the original layout than in Figure A.

concept.rationale: Figure B allows interactive exploration of how 3D velocity inversely scales with depth to produce image velocity, which directly teaches the core mathematical concept of optical flow.

aggregator.explanation: Although Figure A provides a true 3D geometric construction, Figure B strongly outperforms it across all other dimensions. Figure B accurately replicates the original design and labeling, while its interactive sliders effectively demonstrate the core mathematical concepts of optical flow, making it significantly more pedagogically valuable.

geometry.rationale: Figure A incorrectly implements the diagram as a 2D drawing mapped onto a flat plane in 3D space, whereas Figure B accurately reconstructs the true 3D geometry of the camera coordinate system and projection.

interactivity.rationale: Figure B includes a functional slider and toggle button to interactively rotate a ghost point around the different Euler axes, whereas Figure A only features basic hover tooltips.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure A faithfully reproduces the colors of the points (blue), maintains proper label spacing without overlap, and accurately models the rotation arrows and plane proportions, whereas Figure B has incorrect colors, overlapping text, and poorly formed rotation arrows.

labels.rationale: Figure A provides correct label text and clear placement, whereas Figure B includes incorrect labels (x', y') and suffers from overlapping text.

concept.rationale: Figure B constructs a true 3D geometric representation with correct projective relationships and functional interactive rotations, whereas Figure A merely draws 2D shapes on a flat plane, misrepresenting the 3D concepts.

aggregator.explanation: Figure B is the overall winner because it successfully constructs a true 3D geometric representation with functional interactivity, accurately capturing the core mathematical concepts. Although Figure A offers better styling and clearer labels, its fundamental flaw of rendering a 3D diagram as a flat 2D drawing makes it conceptually and geometrically inferior.

geometry.rationale: Figure A builds a true 3D geometric reconstruction with mathematically accurate projection rays and an interactive depth slider, whereas Figure B merely draws a static 2D representation using 3D primitives.

interactivity.rationale: Figure A includes a fully functional, pedagogically highly relevant depth slider that interactively updates the 3D geometry and labels, whereas Figure B only features hover tooltips.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure B correctly includes the self-loop arrow for P'=MP and smooth arrow trajectories, whereas Figure A draws a bizarre outgoing arrow for P, a squiggly line for p'=KP, and includes an unwanted UI slider.

labels.rationale: Figure B much more accurately reproduces the annotation arrows, specifically the looping arrow at point P and the curved arrow to p', which are distorted or incorrectly drawn in Figure A.

concept.rationale: Figure A includes an interactive depth slider that visually demonstrates how changing depth moves the 3D point along the camera ray and shifts its reprojection in the reference frame, perfectly illustrating the underlying geometric concept.

aggregator.explanation: Figure A significantly outperforms Figure B by implementing a true interactive 3D reconstruction with a functional depth slider, excellently illustrating the geometric concept of reprojection. While Figure B is more faithful to the static layout and specific arrow trajectories of the original diagram, Figure A's interactive and conceptual depth makes it the overall superior educational visualization.
